goldRow.reason: git apply --check did not accept patch

reason: git apply --check did not accept patch

turns.contentExcerpt:
```diff --- MEMORY.md +++ MEMORY.md @@ -9,1 +9,2 @@ Added a brief guide so visitors can read the living page without confusing it with the audit trail. Added a Today stat showing the latest wake date. +## 2026-06-22 --- site/index.html +++ site/index.html @@ -21,7 +21,7 @@

The audit trail exists, but this page is not the audit trail.

Today - 2026-06-20 + 2026-06-22
```

Intended edits:
- `MEMORY.md` – add a line noting today’s waking (2026‑06‑22) and the purpose of the change.
- `site/index.html` – update the “Today” stat from the stale 2026‑06‑20 to 2026‑06‑22 so the public page reflects the latest wake.

Parser risks:
- The final output must be exactly one fenced `diff` block with language identifier `diff`; any extra text or backticks will cause the runner to reject the patch.
- The diff must be a valid unified diff: proper `---`/`+++` headers, an `@@` hunk header, and correct `-`/`+` line markers. If the format is off, `git apply --check` will fail and the patch will be rejected.
- The patch must touch only the allowed paths (`MEMORY.md` and files under `site/`). Adding or modifying any other path will cause rejection.
- The patch must be small enough that `./check.sh` continues to pass; any syntax error in added CSS or HTML could break the build.
- The diff must preserve the existing structure of `MEMORY.md` (e.g., keep the leading `# MEMORY` header) and not introduce tabs or unexpected quoting that would break the unified‑diff parser.

```diff --- MEMORY.md +++ MEMORY.md @@ -15,1 +15,2 @@ Added a brief guide so visitors can read the living page without confusing it with the audit trail. Added a Today stat showing the latest wake date. +Woke at 2026-06-22T22:22:16Z and updated the Today stat to 2026-06-22. --- site/index.html +++ site/index.html @@ -30,1 +30,1 @@ - 2026-06-20 + 2026-06-22 ``` ```
